wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the ARHAT POS project overview section. Explain the project's purpose as a cloud-based Point of Sale and business management platform for UMKM, its target audience, core vision and mission statements. Document the problem statement addressing manual transaction recording, inventory mismanagement, customer relationship challenges, and reporting inefficiencies. Describe the core modules including Authentication & User Management, Dashboard Analytics, Point of Sale (POS), Product Management, Inventory Management, Customer Relationship Management (CRM), Supplier Management, Financial Management, Reporting & Analytics, WhatsApp Integration, Multi Outlet Management, and Subscription Management. Include the technology stack overview covering frontend (Next.js, React, TypeScript), backend (Cloudflare Workers, Hono), database (PostgreSQL), storage (Supabase Storage, Cloudflare R2), and DevOps (GitHub, GitHub Actions). Provide success metrics and future roadmap including versions 2.0 through 5.0 with planned feature enhancements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Introduction & Vision
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the ARHAT POS introduction and vision section. Explain the project's mission to digitize small and medium enterprises (UMKM) in Indonesia through a cloud-based Point of Sale and business management platform. Document the vision of transforming traditional business operations into modern, efficient, and scalable systems. Describe the target audience including small retailers, restaurants, and service providers who struggle with manual bookkeeping and inefficient inventory management. Include the core value proposition of reducing operational costs, improving customer experience, and enabling data-driven decision making. Provide context on why this solution is crucial for Indonesia's UMKM sector and how it addresses the digital transformation gap.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for ARHAT POS system overview. Explain the overall system architecture, high-level design principles, and technology stack selection rationale. Document the monorepo structure using pnpm workspace and Turbo for build optimization. Describe the separation between frontend (Next.js web application) and backend (Hono.js API server) components. Include system boundaries, main functional domains, and how different modules interact. Provide architectural diagrams showing the relationship between frontend, backend, database, and external services. Address scalability considerations, performance characteristics, and deployment topology. Use terminology consistent with the codebase and include practical examples of system behavior.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Schema Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for ARHAT POS database schema design. Detail entity relationship diagrams showing relationships between Users, Products, Transactions, Customers, Inventory, RawMaterials, and other core entities. Document field definitions, data types, primary/foreign keys, indexes, and constraints for each table. Explain the normalized database structure and table relationships. Include database schema diagrams and sample data structures. Document data validation rules, business rules, and referential integrity constraints. Address data security, privacy requirements, and access control mechanisms. Explain the rationale behind table designs and normalization choices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the Authentication module. Document all authentication endpoints including POST /api/auth/login for user authentication, POST /api/auth/register for user registration, POST /api/auth/logout for session termination, and GET /api/auth/profile for retrieving authenticated user information. For each endpoint, specify HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, authentication requirements, and error handling. Document JWT token generation, refresh token handling, password hashing, and session management. Include middleware authentication flow, role-based access control, and security considerations. Provide practical examples of login/logout workflows, token validation, and protected route access patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Next.js App Router & File-Based Routing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Next.js App Router implementation in ARHAT POS. Document the file-based routing system, page structure, and route organization patterns. Explain the difference between pages and layouts, nested routing with groups like (admin) and (dashboard). Detail the main application pages including dashboard, POS interface, product management, customer management, reports, settings, transactions, and users. Document route parameters, dynamic routes, and catch-all routes. Explain the root layout configuration, metadata handling, and global styles. Include examples of route protection, redirects, and custom error handling. Address performance optimization through automatic code splitting and route-level caching strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Authentication Flow
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the user authentication flow in ARHAT POS. Detail the complete authentication process including user registration, login via email/password, and PIN-based login. Document the request/response schemas, validation rules, and error handling mechanisms. Explain the authentication controller implementation, service layer logic, and middleware integration. Include step-by-step authentication workflows, security measures during authentication, and IP address tracking for security purposes. Provide code examples showing how to implement each authentication method and handle authentication responses.

wiki_catalogs.name: Product Catalog Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for Product Catalog Management system. Document product creation, modification, and deletion workflows including product metadata, descriptions, categories, and classifications. Explain product variants management with size, color, SKU variations, and pricing strategies. Detail barcode integration for product identification and scanning. Document product image management, bulk operations, and import/export capabilities. Include product search functionality, filtering, and sorting mechanisms. Explain product status management (active, inactive, discontinued). Provide practical examples of product CRUD operations, variant creation, and barcode scanning integration. Address product validation rules, duplicate detection, and data consistency measures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Checkout Interface & User Experience
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the POS checkout interface and user experience. Document the main POS page layout, product grid display, and product search functionality including barcode scanning integration. Explain the shopping cart panel implementation with item management, quantity adjustment, and total calculation. Detail the user interface components including ProductGrid, ProductSearch, CartPanel, and POSHeaderActions. Document the Zustand-based cart state management system including add/remove items, quantity updates, and cart persistence. Explain the responsive design considerations, keyboard shortcuts, and accessibility features. Include user workflow patterns from product selection through checkout completion. Document error handling for out-of-stock items, invalid quantities, and system failures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Customer Profile Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for customer profile management in ARHAT POS CRM system. Document customer registration and onboarding workflows including personal information collection, contact verification, and initial customer setup. Explain customer data fields including names, contact details, addresses, and demographic information. Detail customer segmentation strategies for different customer types such as walk-in customers, registered customers, and VIP clients. Document customer search and filtering capabilities, duplicate detection, and data validation rules. Include customer profile editing, update workflows, and data privacy controls. Address customer data import/export functionality and integration with external customer databases. Explain customer lifecycle stages and automated customer classification based on purchase behavior.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dashboard Analytics
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive dashboard analytics documentation for ARHAT POS KPI monitoring system. Document the main dashboard metrics including daily sales revenue, total transactions count, monthly recurring revenue, product inventory value, customer acquisition metrics, and staff performance indicators. Explain the dashboard data aggregation logic, real-time updates mechanism, and caching strategy using memoryCache. Detail the dashboard API endpoints and their response formats. Include configuration options for metric thresholds, alert triggers, and custom KPI definitions. Document the frontend dashboard implementation using Recharts for data visualization, responsive design patterns, and mobile optimization. Address performance considerations for large datasets, data refresh intervals, and cache invalidation strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Cloud Deployment
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive cloud deployment documentation for ARHAT POS. Document the serverless deployment strategy using Cloudflare Workers for the backend API and Vercel for the frontend application. Explain the benefits of serverless architecture including auto-scaling, cost optimization, and reduced infrastructure management overhead. Detail the deployment configuration in wrangler.toml for Cloudflare Workers including environment variables, secrets management, and routing rules. Document Vercel deployment configuration in vercel.json for frontend optimization, custom domains, and preview deployments. Include step-by-step deployment procedures, environment setup, and domain configuration. Explain the separation of concerns between frontend and backend deployment, CDN optimization, and edge computing benefits. Address deployment troubleshooting, rollback procedures, and monitoring setup for deployed applications.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for ARHAT POS. Include prerequisites and system requirements, detailed installation and setup instructions for both development and production environments. Document the monorepo structure using pnpm workspace and Turbo for build optimization. Provide step-by-step instructions for setting up the development environment, configuring environment variables, running the application locally, and accessing different modules (API server and web frontend). Include initial user setup, default credentials, and first-time configuration steps. Document the project structure, key configuration files (drizzle.config.ts, wrangler.toml, vercel.json), and essential commands for development workflow. Address common setup issues and provide troubleshooting guidance for environment configuration problems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Problem Statement
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the ARHAT POS problem statement section. Detail the critical challenges facing Indonesian UMKM businesses including manual transaction recording leading to errors and lost sales, inventory mismanagement causing stockouts and overstock situations, difficulty in tracking customer purchase history and preferences, inefficient reporting processes that delay decision-making, lack of real-time business insights, poor customer relationship management, limited integration with modern payment methods, absence of multi-outlet coordination, and inadequate financial tracking capabilities. Explain how these problems collectively impact business growth, profitability, and competitiveness. Include statistics and real-world scenarios illustrating the severity of these challenges in the UMKM sector.

wiki_catalogs.name: Monorepo Structure
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for ARHAT POS monorepo structure and workspace management. Explain the pnpm workspace configuration and how it enables shared dependencies across applications. Document the Turbo build system setup for optimizing builds and caching. Detail the packages directory structure including shared UI components, TypeScript configurations, and ESLint rules. Explain how the monorepo architecture benefits development workflow, code sharing, and dependency management. Include workspace configuration examples, build commands, and development patterns. Address workspace limitations and best practices for maintaining consistency across packages.

wiki_catalogs.name: Migration Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for ARHAT POS database migration management using Drizzle ORM. Explain the migration workflow, creation of new migrations, and execution procedures. Document the migration file structure, naming conventions, and version control. Detail the migration execution process, rollback procedures, and error handling. Include practical examples of creating schema changes, data migrations, and constraint modifications. Explain the relationship between migration files and database state management. Document best practices for migration development, testing, and deployment across different environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Product Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for the Product Management module. Document all product-related endpoints including GET /api/products for product listing with filtering and pagination, POST /api/products for product creation, GET /api/products/:id for individual product retrieval, PUT /api/products/:id for product updates, DELETE /api/products/:id for product deletion, and GET /api/products/:id/variants for product variant management. Document product variant handling, pricing calculations, barcode integration, image upload endpoints, and stock level synchronization. Include request validation schemas, error handling for product conflicts, and bulk operations. Provide examples of product CRUD operations, variant management workflows, and integration with inventory systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Architecture & UI Library
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the frontend component architecture in ARHAT POS. Document the component hierarchy starting with layout components like DashboardLayout, feature-specific components for POS, products, and users, and shared UI components. Explain the component composition patterns, prop interfaces, and component reusability strategies. Detail the UI library integration with Tailwind CSS and shadcn/ui components. Document the modal system, form components, and specialized POS components like ProductGrid, CartPanel, and ReceiptTemplate. Explain component state management, event handling, and data binding patterns. Include guidelines for creating new components, component testing strategies, and accessibility considerations. Address component performance optimization and lazy loading patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) system in ARHAT POS. Document the different user roles including Super Admin, Owner, Manager, and Cashier with their specific permissions and access levels. Explain the role assignment process, permission checking mechanisms, and authorization middleware implementation. Detail how roles are stored, validated, and enforced throughout the application. Include examples of role-based route protection, component rendering based on permissions, and API endpoint access control. Document the relationship between user roles and tenant management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Inventory Control System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for Inventory Control System. Document stock tracking mechanisms including stock in, stock out, stock adjustments, and transfers between locations. Explain inventory valuation methods, cost calculations, and COGS (Cost of Goods Sold) tracking. Detail low stock alerts, reorder point management, and automated reordering workflows. Document physical inventory counting procedures, cycle counting, and inventory reconciliation processes. Explain batch tracking, expiry date management, and FIFO/LIFO inventory costing methods. Include inventory movement tracking, audit trails, and inventory reporting capabilities. Provide practical examples of stock adjustment procedures, inventory transfer workflows, and automated reorder triggers. Address inventory accuracy measures, shrinkage tracking, and inventory variance analysis.

wiki_catalogs.name: Payment Processing & Methods
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for payment processing and methods in the POS system. Document supported payment methods including cash, QRIS, bank transfer, debit cards, credit cards, and e-wallets. Explain the payment modal implementation for non-cash transactions including amount input, payment method selection, and confirmation workflows. Detail the transaction service layer handling payment validation, amount processing, and payment method integration. Document the payment workflow from initiation through completion, including partial payments, change calculation, and payment failure handling. Explain security measures for payment data handling, PCI compliance considerations, and payment method validation. Include integration patterns with external payment processors and webhook handling for asynchronous payment confirmation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Purchase History & Transaction Analysis
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for purchase history tracking and transaction analysis in ARHAT POS CRM. Document transaction recording, customer purchase history maintenance, and purchase pattern analysis. Explain customer spending behavior tracking, frequency analysis, and average transaction value calculations. Detail purchase history filtering by date ranges, product categories, and transaction types. Document customer purchase journey mapping, product preference analysis, and cross-selling opportunities identification. Include customer lifetime value (CLV) calculations, retention rate analysis, and customer profitability assessment. Address purchase history reporting, trend analysis, and seasonal buying pattern recognition. Explain integration with POS transactions and real-time purchase data synchronization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Sales Analytics
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed sales analytics documentation for ARHAT POS revenue tracking and analysis system. Document sales reporting capabilities including daily sales trends, weekly and monthly sales patterns, seasonal analysis, and year-over-year comparisons. Explain sales data aggregation by product categories, sales channels, and geographic regions. Detail sales forecasting algorithms, revenue projections, and sales target tracking. Include sales performance metrics such as average transaction value, conversion rates, and sales growth rates. Document filtering options by date ranges, product categories, sales representatives, and customer segments. Explain export functionality for sales reports in PDF, Excel, and CSV formats. Address real-time sales monitoring, automated sales alerts, and integration with accounting systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Containerization & Local Development
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed containerization documentation for ARHAT POS development environment. Document the Docker Compose configuration for orchestrating development containers including database services, API server, and frontend application. Explain the container networking setup, volume mounting for persistent data, and environment variable management. Detail the local development workflow using Docker containers for consistent development environments across team members. Include container build processes, image optimization strategies, and multi-stage builds. Document debugging techniques within containers, log aggregation, and health checks. Explain the transition from containerized development to cloud deployment, including configuration differences and migration strategies. Address container security best practices, resource limits, and performance optimization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture & Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for ARHAT POS system design. Describe the high-level architecture including the monorepo structure with separate API and web applications. Document the layered architecture pattern with clear separation between presentation, business logic, and data access layers. Explain the microservices-like structure where each major feature has its own controller, service, and route modules. Detail the serverless deployment strategy using Cloudflare Workers for the backend API and Vercel for the frontend. Document the database architecture using Drizzle ORM with PostgreSQL, caching strategies with Cloudflare KV, and storage solutions. Include system boundaries, component interactions, data flows, and integration patterns. Address cross-cutting concerns like security, monitoring, and scalability considerations. Provide system context diagrams and component breakdowns showing the relationship between frontend, backend, database, and external services.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Modules & Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the ARHAT POS core modules and features section. Document the complete feature set organized into major modules: Authentication & User Management (user registration, login, role-based access control), Dashboard Analytics (real-time business metrics, KPI tracking), Point of Sale (POS interface, payment processing, receipt generation), Product Management (product catalog, variants, pricing, barcode integration), Inventory Management (stock tracking, supplier integration, alerts), Customer Relationship Management (customer profiles, purchase history, communication), Supplier Management (vendor tracking, procurement), Financial Management (revenue tracking, expenses, cash flow), Reporting & Analytics (sales reports, inventory analytics, business insights), WhatsApp Integration (automated receipts, customer communication), Multi Outlet Management (branch coordination, centralized control), and Subscription Management (business plans, billing). For each module, describe key functionalities, user workflows, and integration points with other modules.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for ARHAT POS deployment architecture and cloud infrastructure. Document the serverless deployment strategy using Cloudflare Workers for the backend API, including wrangler configuration and environment variables. Explain the frontend deployment on Vercel with static site generation and edge network optimization. Detail the Docker containerization approach for local development and testing environments. Document the CI/CD pipeline configuration, automated deployments, and environment management. Include infrastructure diagrams showing the relationship between Cloudflare Workers, Vercel, databases, and CDN services. Address scaling strategies, load balancing, and disaster recovery considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Models & Services
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for ARHAT POS data models and service layer integration. Detail how Drizzle ORM models map to database tables and handle CRUD operations. Document the service layer architecture that encapsulates business logic and data access patterns. Explain the relationship between models, services, and controllers in the layered architecture. Include examples of complex queries, joins, and data transformations. Document caching strategies, query optimization techniques, and performance considerations. Address data validation, error handling, and transaction management throughout the data access layer.

wiki_catalogs.name: Transaction Processing API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the Transaction Processing module. Document all transaction endpoints including POST /api/transactions for creating new sales transactions, GET /api/transactions for transaction history with filtering, GET /api/transactions/:id for transaction details, POST /api/transactions/:id/void for transaction cancellation, and GET /api/transactions/report for sales reporting. Document payment processing integration for cash, card, and digital payments, receipt generation endpoints, transaction status management, and real-time checkout workflows. Include inventory deduction logic, tax calculation, discount application, and multi-item transaction handling. Provide examples of complete checkout flows, payment processing scenarios, and transaction reporting patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: State Management with Zustand
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for state management implementation using Zustand in ARHAT POS. Document the cart store implementation including product selection, quantity management, and checkout state. Explain the authentication context for user session management and global state coordination. Detail store creation patterns, actions, and selectors. Document state persistence strategies, state hydration, and cross-component state sharing. Explain middleware usage for logging, persistence, and async operations. Include examples of store composition, derived state calculations, and state subscription patterns. Address state debugging techniques, performance optimization, and memory management considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: JWT Token Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for JWT token management in ARHAT POS. Explain the token generation process, payload structure, and expiration policies. Document the refresh token mechanism, token validation procedures, and security considerations. Detail the token storage strategies, automatic renewal processes, and logout functionality. Include implementation examples for token creation, verification, and invalidation. Address token security best practices, potential vulnerabilities, and mitigation strategies. Document the integration between authentication service and middleware for token validation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Supplier & Raw Materials Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for Supplier & Raw Materials Management system. Document supplier relationship management including supplier registration, evaluation, and performance tracking. Explain raw materials procurement processes, purchase order management, and goods receiving procedures. Detail supplier pricing management, contract terms, and payment workflows. Document raw material categorization, specification tracking, and quality control processes. Explain supplier delivery schedules, lead time management, and supply chain coordination. Include supplier scorecards, performance metrics, and relationship optimization strategies. Provide practical examples of supplier onboarding, purchase order creation, and goods receipt validation. Address supplier risk management, alternative sourcing strategies, and supplier communication workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: WhatsApp Communication Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for WhatsApp integration in ARHAT POS CRM system. Document automated receipt delivery via WhatsApp, customer notification systems, and promotional message campaigns. Explain WhatsApp API integration, message templates, and personalized customer communication workflows. Detail order status updates, delivery notifications, and customer service automation through WhatsApp. Document WhatsApp business profile setup, message scheduling, and broadcast messaging capabilities. Include customer opt-in/opt-out management, consent tracking, and compliance with WhatsApp messaging regulations. Address WhatsApp template approval process, message formatting, and rich media content integration. Explain integration with POS transactions for automatic order confirmation and delivery notifications.

wiki_catalogs.name: Receipt Generation & Delivery
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for receipt generation and delivery systems. Document the receipt template system including header, itemized listings, totals calculation, tax breakdown, and footer information. Explain receipt generation workflows triggered by successful transactions, including print job creation and PDF export functionality. Detail the multi-channel receipt delivery system supporting email, WhatsApp, and physical print outputs. Document the WhatsApp receipt integration including template customization, automated sending, and delivery status tracking. Explain receipt formatting options, localization support, and custom branding capabilities. Include receipt storage, retrieval, and reprint functionality for customer service scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Product Analytics
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive product analytics documentation for ARHAT POS inventory and product performance analysis. Document product reporting features including best-selling products analysis, slow-moving inventory identification, product category performance, and stock turnover rates. Explain product profitability analysis, margin tracking, and cost of goods sold calculations. Detail product lifecycle analytics, seasonal demand patterns, and product adoption curves. Include inventory analytics covering stock levels, reorder point tracking, and supply chain efficiency metrics. Document product mix analysis, cross-selling opportunities, and product bundling recommendations. Explain product performance dashboards, SKU-level reporting, and supplier performance metrics. Address product recommendation algorithms, demand forecasting, and markdown optimization strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: CI/CD Pipeline & Automation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive CI/CD pipeline documentation for ARHAT POS automation workflows. Document the GitHub Actions configuration for automated testing, building, and deployment processes. Explain the pipeline stages including code quality checks, unit testing, integration testing, and deployment to different environments. Detail the monorepo build optimization using Turbo for parallel execution and caching strategies. Document environment-specific deployment triggers, branch protection rules, and release management workflows. Include automated dependency updates, security scanning, and vulnerability assessment processes. Explain the deployment strategy for multiple environments (development, staging, production) with proper gating and approval workflows. Address pipeline monitoring, failure notifications, and manual intervention procedures. Document rollback automation and deployment verification processes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend API Documentation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for ARHAT POS backend services. Document all RESTful API endpoints organized by functional modules including Authentication (/api/auth), Product Management (/api/products), Transaction Processing (/api/transactions), Analytics (/api/analytics), Inventory (/api/inventory), Customers (/api/customers), Users (/api/users), and WhatsApp Integration (/api/whatsapp). For each endpoint, specify HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, authentication requirements, and error handling. Document the middleware stack including authentication, error handling, and request validation. Explain the service layer architecture with business logic encapsulation and data access patterns. Include API versioning strategy, rate limiting, and security considerations. Provide practical examples of API usage, common request/response patterns, and integration guidelines for frontend applications.

wiki_catalogs.name: Technology Stack
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the ARHAT POS technology stack section. Document the complete technology architecture including frontend technologies (Next.js 14+ with App Router, React 18, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Zustand for state management), backend technologies (Cloudflare Workers serverless platform, Hono.js framework, Express-compatible routing), database layer (PostgreSQL with Drizzle ORM, SQLite for development), storage solutions (Supabase Storage, Cloudflare R2 for media), DevOps infrastructure (GitHub for version control, GitHub Actions for CI/CD, Docker for containerization), development tools (ESLint, Prettier, Husky for git hooks, Turbo for monorepo optimization), and deployment platforms (Cloudflare Workers for backend, Vercel for frontend). Explain the rationale behind each technology choice, benefits, and integration patterns between components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Flow & Processing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for ARHAT POS data flow architecture and processing patterns. Document the layered architecture with clear separation between presentation, business logic, and data access layers. Explain request/response flow from frontend components through API controllers to service layer and database operations. Detail the middleware pipeline for authentication, error handling, and request preprocessing. Include data transformation patterns, caching strategies, and state management approaches. Document the integration patterns with external services like WhatsApp API and image storage. Provide sequence diagrams showing typical data processing workflows and error handling scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Initialization & Seeding
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for ARHAT POS database initialization and data seeding procedures. Explain the database setup process, schema creation, and initial data population. Document the seed scripts that populate default data like admin users, categories, and system configurations. Detail the database cleanup and reset procedures using drop-schema scripts. Include environment-specific initialization for development, staging, and production. Document backup and restore procedures, database health checks, and maintenance tasks. Address database connection management, pooling, and configuration for different deployment environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Analytics & Reporting API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for the Analytics and Reporting module. Document all analytics endpoints including GET /api/analytics/sales for sales performance metrics, GET /api/analytics/inventory for inventory turnover reports, GET /api/analytics/customers for customer behavior analytics, GET /api/analytics/revenue for financial summaries, and GET /api/analytics/top-products for product performance rankings. Document date range filtering, aggregation functions, data visualization formats, and export capabilities. Include real-time dashboard metrics, historical trend analysis, and custom report generation. Provide examples of report generation workflows, data filtering patterns, and integration with business intelligence tools.

wiki_catalogs.name: Progressive Web App & Mobile Optimization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for Progressive Web App functionality and mobile optimization in ARHAT POS. Document the service worker implementation for offline capabilities, background sync, and push notification handling. Explain the PWA registration process, manifest configuration, and app installation prompts. Detail offline data synchronization strategies, IndexedDB usage for local storage, and conflict resolution. Document mobile-specific optimizations including touch interactions, responsive design patterns, and device-specific features. Explain the icon system, splash screens, and PWA manifest configuration. Include guidelines for testing PWA functionality, performance optimization for mobile devices, and troubleshooting common PWA issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Authentication Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for frontend authentication integration in ARHAT POS. Detail the AuthContext implementation, state management for authenticated users, and session persistence. Document the authentication flow in the Next.js frontend, including login page implementation, protected route handling, and navigation guards. Explain how the frontend communicates with backend authentication APIs, handles authentication responses, and manages user sessions. Include examples of protected component rendering, authentication state updates, and error handling in the frontend. Address PWA authentication considerations and offline access patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Stock Monitoring & Alerts
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for Stock Monitoring & Alerts system. Document real-time stock level monitoring, automated alert triggers, and notification mechanisms. Explain low stock warnings, stockout prevention, and safety stock calculation methods. Detail inventory aging reports, slow-moving item identification, and obsolete inventory tracking. Document stock utilization analysis, demand forecasting integration, and inventory turnover metrics. Explain customizable alert thresholds, escalation procedures, and stakeholder notification workflows. Include dashboard widgets, report generation, and trend analysis capabilities. Provide practical examples of setting up stock alerts, configuring notification preferences, and interpreting inventory analytics. Address alert fatigue prevention, intelligent threshold tuning, and integration with warehouse management systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Loyalty & Rewards Program
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for loyalty and rewards program implementation in ARHAT POS CRM. Document reward point calculation algorithms, point accumulation rules, and redemption systems. Explain membership tiers, status-based benefits, and progression criteria. Detail cashback programs, discount structures, and promotional offer management. Document voucher generation, coupon systems, and special offer distribution through WhatsApp. Include loyalty program analytics, member engagement tracking, and program effectiveness measurement. Address point expiration policies, transfer mechanisms, and integration with purchase transactions. Explain customer tier upgrades, downgrade scenarios, and loyalty program customization options.

wiki_catalogs.name: Transaction Workflows & States
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for transaction workflows and state management. Document transaction lifecycle including creation, modification, completion, void, and refund states. Explain held transaction functionality for paused transactions, reservation systems, and transaction recovery. Detail shift-based transaction management including shift opening/closing, cash handling, and end-of-day reconciliation. Document transaction auditing including creation timestamps, modification logs, user attribution, and system events. Explain transaction validation rules, constraint checking, and business logic enforcement. Include error handling for concurrent transactions, system failures, and data consistency maintenance. Document transaction reporting capabilities and historical data management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Customer Analytics
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed customer analytics documentation for ARHAT POS customer behavior and relationship analysis system. Document customer reporting features including top customer identification, customer lifetime value calculations, repeat purchase analysis, and customer segmentation. Explain customer acquisition cost tracking, customer retention rates, and churn prediction models. Detail customer behavior analytics including purchase frequency, average order value, product preferences, and shopping cart abandonment analysis. Include customer satisfaction metrics, Net Promoter Score tracking, and customer feedback analysis. Document customer journey mapping, attribution modeling, and marketing campaign effectiveness measurement. Explain predictive analytics for customer behavior, personalized recommendations, and targeted marketing campaigns. Address customer data privacy compliance, consent management, and data governance practices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Infrastructure & Environment Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed infrastructure and environment management documentation for ARHAT POS. Document the infrastructure provisioning strategy including database setup, CDN configuration, and external service integrations. Explain environment configuration management for development, staging, and production environments with proper separation of concerns. Detail the database infrastructure including Drizzle ORM configuration, migration management, and connection pooling. Document external service integrations such as WhatsApp API, email services, and cloud storage. Include infrastructure monitoring setup, alerting configuration, and performance metrics collection. Explain backup strategies, disaster recovery procedures, and maintenance workflows. Address security considerations including SSL/TLS certificates, firewall configuration, and access control policies. Document capacity planning, scaling strategies, and cost optimization techniques for cloud resources.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Application Guide
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed frontend application documentation for ARHAT POS built with Next.js. Document the app router structure, file-based routing system, and page organization. Explain the component architecture including reusable UI components, layout components, and feature-specific components. Detail the state management implementation using Zustand for cart and application state. Document the authentication context and global state management patterns. Explain the PWA functionality including service worker configuration, offline capabilities, and mobile optimization. Cover the UI component library integration with Tailwind CSS and shadcn/ui. Document the API integration patterns, data fetching strategies, and error handling. Include component composition patterns, prop interfaces, and integration guidelines for extending the frontend functionality.

wiki_catalogs.name: Success Metrics & Roadmap
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the ARHAT POS success metrics and roadmap section. Document the key performance indicators including user adoption rates, transaction volume growth, customer retention metrics, system uptime and reliability measures, and business efficiency improvements. Detail the implementation roadmap from version 1.0 through 5.0, explaining the phased development approach and feature prioritization. Include the timeline for major releases, feature milestones, and technical debt reduction goals. Document the success criteria for each phase, including user feedback mechanisms, performance benchmarks, and business impact measurements. Explain how the roadmap aligns with market needs, competitive landscape, and technological evolution in the UMKM space.

wiki_catalogs.name: Security Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for ARHAT POS security architecture and protection mechanisms. Document the authentication and authorization system including JWT token management, session handling, and role-based access control. Explain input validation, sanitization, and protection against common vulnerabilities like XSS, CSRF, and SQL injection. Detail the middleware-based security approach and error handling strategies. Include security headers configuration, CORS policies, and secure communication protocols. Document data encryption at rest and in transit, password hashing mechanisms, and audit logging. Address security monitoring, threat detection, and incident response procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Inventory Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the Inventory Management module. Document all inventory endpoints including GET /api/inventory for stock level monitoring, POST /api/inventory/adjustments for stock corrections, GET /api/inventory/alerts for low-stock notifications, POST /api/inventory/transfers for internal movement, and GET /api/inventory/history for audit trails. Document raw material tracking, supplier integration, batch management, expiration date handling, and automated reorder point calculations. Include inventory valuation methods, cost of goods sold tracking, and multi-location inventory support. Provide examples of inventory workflows, stock adjustment procedures, and integration with procurement systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Integration & Data Fetching
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for API integration patterns and data fetching strategies in ARHAT POS. Document the API client implementation, request/response handling, and error management. Explain the middleware patterns for authentication, request transformation, and response processing. Detail the internationalization (i18n) system for multi-language support and locale management. Document the barcode scanner integration hook and hardware interaction patterns. Explain data fetching strategies including server-side rendering, client-side fetching, and caching mechanisms. Include examples of API error handling, retry logic, and loading state management. Address performance optimization through request batching, caching strategies, and optimistic updates.

wiki_catalogs.name: Customer Communication Strategies
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for customer communication strategies within ARHAT POS CRM system. Document multi-channel communication approaches including email, WhatsApp, SMS, and in-app notifications. Explain customer preference management, communication channel selection, and message personalization. Detail automated communication workflows for welcome messages, purchase confirmations, abandoned cart reminders, and promotional offers. Document customer feedback collection, satisfaction surveys, and complaint resolution processes. Include communication analytics, response rate tracking, and customer sentiment analysis. Address compliance with anti-spam regulations, opt-out management, and data protection requirements. Explain integration with transaction events for timely and relevant customer communications.

wiki_catalogs.name: Discounts, Taxes & Calculations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for discount application, tax calculation, and financial computations. Document discount mechanisms including percentage discounts, fixed amount discounts, buy-one-get-one offers, and bulk purchase discounts. Explain tax calculation automation including tax rate application, tax exemption handling, and multi-tax jurisdiction support. Detail the calculation engine for real-time price updates, discount application timing, and final amount computation. Document special pricing scenarios including member discounts, promotional pricing, and dynamic pricing adjustments. Explain rounding policies, currency handling, and internationalization considerations. Include validation rules for discount codes, tax exemptions, and calculation accuracy verification.

wiki_catalogs.name: Financial Analytics
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive financial analytics documentation for ARHAT POS accounting and financial performance reporting system. Document profit and loss statement generation, revenue analysis by product and service lines, and expense tracking across departments. Explain cash flow monitoring, accounts receivable analysis, and accounts payable optimization. Detail financial ratio calculations including gross profit margin, net profit margin, return on investment, and debt-to-equity ratios. Include budget vs actual variance analysis, forecasting models, and financial trend analysis. Document tax reporting capabilities, accrual vs cash basis accounting, and consolidated financial statements. Explain financial dashboard creation, automated financial alerts, and regulatory reporting requirements. Address integration with external accounting systems, real-time financial data feeds, and compliance reporting automation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Design & Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for ARHAT POS database design. Detail the entity relationship diagrams showing relationships between Users, Products, Transactions, Customers, Inventory, and other core entities. Document field definitions, data types, primary/foreign keys, indexes, and constraints for each table. Explain the database schema evolution through migration management using Drizzle ORM. Document data validation rules, business rules, and referential integrity constraints. Include database schema diagrams and sample data structures. Explain data access patterns, caching strategies, and performance considerations. Specify data lifecycle, retention policies, and archival rules. Document data migration paths, version management, and rollback procedures. Address data security, privacy requirements, and access control mechanisms. Include database initialization scripts, seeding data, and maintenance procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Customer Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for the Customer Management module. Document all customer endpoints including GET /api/customers for customer directory with search and filtering, POST /api/customers for customer registration, GET /api/customers/:id for customer profile retrieval, PUT /api/customers/:id for customer updates, DELETE /api/customers/:id for customer deactivation, and GET /api/customers/:id/transactions for purchase history. Document customer segmentation, loyalty program integration, communication preferences, and purchase behavior tracking. Include address management, contact information updates, and customer lifetime value calculations. Provide examples of customer onboarding workflows, profile management patterns, and CRM integration scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication & Authorization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed authentication and authorization documentation for ARHAT POS. Document the user authentication flow including login, registration, password reset, and email verification processes. Explain the role-based access control (RBAC) system with Super Admin, Owner, Manager, and Cashier roles and their respective permissions. Detail the JWT token implementation, refresh token mechanism, and session management strategies. Document the authentication middleware and protected route handling. Explain security best practices including password hashing, token expiration, and audit logging. Include the frontend authentication context implementation and state management. Document integration with external authentication providers and multi-factor authentication capabilities. Address common security vulnerabilities and mitigation strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Administration API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the User Administration module. Document all user management endpoints including GET /api/users for employee directory with role filtering, POST /api/users for user creation, GET /api/users/:id for user profile details, PUT /api/users/:id for user updates, DELETE /api/users/:id for user deactivation, and POST /api/users/:id/password for password changes. Document role-based access control, permission management, employee scheduling integration, and audit trail functionality. Include user authentication logs, session management, and security clearance levels. Provide examples of user onboarding procedures, role assignment workflows, and administrative access patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Product & Inventory Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Product & Inventory Management system. Document the product catalog management including product creation, updates, deletion, and categorization. Explain product variants, pricing strategies, and barcode integration. Detail the inventory control system covering stock in, stock out, stock adjustment, transfers, and physical inventory counts. Document the raw materials management for products with multiple components. Explain stock monitoring, low stock alerts, and inventory tracking mechanisms. Include supplier integration, purchase order management, and goods receiving processes. Document the relationship between products, variants, and inventory levels. Provide practical examples of inventory workflows, barcode scanning integration, and stock adjustment procedures. Address inventory valuation, cost of goods sold calculations, and inventory reporting.

wiki_catalogs.name: WhatsApp Integration API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for the WhatsApp Integration module. Document all WhatsApp endpoints including POST /api/whatsapp/send-receipt for automated receipt delivery, POST /api/whatsapp/send-message for customer communication, GET /api/whatsapp/status for delivery confirmation, and POST /api/whatsapp/webhook for incoming message handling. Document Twilio WhatsApp API integration, message templating, customer opt-in/opt-out management, and automated messaging workflows. Include receipt generation templates, promotional message scheduling, and customer service automation. Provide examples of receipt delivery workflows, customer communication patterns, and webhook processing scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: POS Transaction Processing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for POS Transaction Processing system. Document the checkout interface design including product search, shopping cart functionality, and transaction management. Explain the payment processing integration supporting cash, QRIS, bank transfer, debit cards, credit cards, and e-wallets. Detail the receipt generation system including print, email, and WhatsApp receipt delivery. Document transaction states including held transactions, resumed transactions, refunds, and void operations. Explain discount application mechanisms for individual items and entire transactions. Detail tax calculation automation and receipt formatting. Include barcode scanner integration, product lookup, and inventory deduction during transactions. Document transaction history, reporting, and reconciliation processes. Address transaction security, audit trails, and error handling during payment processing.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Utilities API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the System Utilities module. Document all utility endpoints including POST /api/upload for file uploads, GET /api/settings for system configuration retrieval, and PUT /api/settings for configuration updates. Document image upload handling, file storage integration, backup restoration, and system maintenance operations. Include CORS configuration, file validation rules, storage quotas, and cleanup procedures. Provide examples of file upload workflows, configuration management patterns, and system administration tasks.

wiki_catalogs.name: Customer Relationship Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive CRM documentation for ARHAT POS customer management system. Document customer profile management including personal information, contact details, and customer segmentation. Explain purchase history tracking, transaction analysis, and customer behavior insights. Detail the WhatsApp integration for automated receipt delivery, customer notifications, promotional messages, and order status updates. Document customer notes, communication history, and relationship building tools. Explain loyalty program implementation including reward points, membership levels, cashback programs, and voucher systems. Include customer retention strategies, repeat customer identification, and customer lifetime value calculations. Document customer data privacy, consent management, and data protection measures. Address customer onboarding workflows and integration with POS transactions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Analytics & Reporting
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed analytics and reporting documentation for ARHAT POS business intelligence system. Document the dashboard design and key performance indicators including daily sales, monthly revenue, total transactions, product counts, customer metrics, and inventory statistics. Explain sales reporting covering daily, weekly, monthly, and annual sales analysis. Detail product reporting including best-selling products, slow-moving inventory, and inventory turnover analysis. Document customer reporting featuring top customers, repeat customer analysis, and customer retention metrics. Explain financial reporting including profit and loss statements, revenue analysis, expense tracking, and cash flow monitoring. Include export functionality for PDF, Excel, and CSV formats. Document real-time dashboard updates, data visualization using Recharts, and custom report generation. Address data aggregation, filtering capabilities, and drill-down reporting features.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Administration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive user administration documentation for ARHAT POS user management system. Document the user management interface for creating, updating, and deactivating user accounts. Explain the role and permission system with Super Admin, Owner, Manager, and Cashier role definitions and access controls. Detail employee scheduling features, shift tracking, and time-based access restrictions. Document user onboarding procedures, role assignment, and permission configuration. Explain the user interface for managing team members, setting up user profiles, and configuring access levels. Include user activity monitoring, audit logs, and user session management. Document integration with POS operations, transaction authorizations, and role-based feature access. Address user training workflows, access revocation procedures, and user lifecycle management.

wiki_catalogs.name: PWA & Mobile Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for Progressive Web App (PWA) and mobile features in ARHAT POS. Document the PWA implementation including service worker configuration, offline functionality, and mobile app installation. Explain the offline capability implementation for critical POS operations, data synchronization, and cached content management. Detail mobile optimization strategies including responsive design, touch-friendly interfaces, and mobile-specific features. Document the service worker lifecycle, background sync, and push notification capabilities. Explain the installation process for iOS Safari and Android Chrome browsers. Include offline data persistence, IndexedDB usage, and conflict resolution during synchronization. Document mobile barcode scanning integration, device-specific optimizations, and performance considerations for mobile devices. Address PWA manifest configuration, app icons, splash screens, and progressive enhancement strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment & DevOps
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive deployment and DevOps documentation for ARHAT POS. Document the cloud deployment strategy using Cloudflare Workers for the backend API and Vercel for the frontend application. Explain the serverless architecture benefits, scaling characteristics, and cost optimization. Detail the Docker containerization approach for local development and potential production deployment. Document the CI/CD pipeline configuration using GitHub Actions for automated testing, building, and deployment. Explain environment configuration management for development, staging, and production environments. Include infrastructure provisioning, database setup, and external service integrations. Document monitoring and logging strategies, error tracking, and performance monitoring. Address backup procedures, disaster recovery planning, and maintenance workflows. Include deployment rollback procedures, blue-green deployment strategies, and zero-downtime deployment techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Testing & Quality Assurance
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive testing and quality assurance documentation for ARHAT POS development workflow. Document the unit testing approach using Jest or similar frameworks for backend services and frontend components. Explain integration testing strategies for API endpoints, database operations, and external service integrations. Detail end-to-end testing using tools like Cypress or Playwright for user workflows and critical business processes. Document code quality standards including ESLint configuration, Prettier formatting, and TypeScript strict mode enforcement. Explain testing best practices, test data management, and continuous integration testing. Include performance testing methodologies, load testing strategies, and security testing procedures. Document bug tracking, issue management, and quality metrics collection. Address testing automation, mock strategies, and test environment management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Guidelines
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive development guidelines documentation for ARHAT POS contributor workflow. Document code style standards including ESLint rules, Prettier formatting configuration, and TypeScript coding conventions. Explain the Git workflow including branching strategies, commit message conventions, and pull request procedures. Detail the code review process, approval requirements, and quality gates. Document the monorepo development workflow using pnpm workspace and Turbo for build optimization. Explain dependency management, versioning strategies, and release procedures. Include performance optimization guidelines, security coding practices, and accessibility standards. Document debugging techniques, profiling tools, and development environment setup. Address contribution guidelines, issue reporting, and community collaboration practices. Include onboarding procedures for new developers and mentorship resources.

wiki_catalogs.name: Troubleshooting & FAQ
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive troubleshooting and FAQ documentation for ARHAT POS users and developers. Document common issues and their solutions including installation problems, configuration errors, and runtime exceptions. Explain debugging techniques for frontend components, backend API issues, and database connectivity problems. Detail performance optimization tips including database query optimization, caching strategies, and frontend performance improvements. Document error handling patterns, logging strategies, and diagnostic procedures. Include frequently asked questions covering user onboarding, feature usage, and system limitations. Address common integration issues with external services, payment processors, and hardware devices. Document support resources, community forums, and escalation procedures. Include system health monitoring, alerting strategies, and incident response procedures. Provide step-by-step troubleshooting guides for critical system failures and recovery procedures.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# ARHAT POS - Comprehensive Project Analysis

## 1. Project Introduction

### Purpose Statement
ARHAT POS is a modern Point of Sale (POS) system designed for retail environments, featuring real-time inventory management, customer transaction processing, and comprehensive reporting capabilities. The system provides both web-based administration and mobile POS functionality for seamless business operations.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Real-time Inventory Management**: Track stock levels, product variants, and raw materials with automated updates
- **Multi-channel Sales Processing**: Support both in-store and online sales through integrated POS system
- **Comprehensive Reporting**: Generate detailed analytics on sales trends, inventory turnover, and revenue metrics
- **User-friendly Interface**: Provide intuitive dashboards for administrators and efficient checkout experience for cashiers
- **Scalable Architecture**: Built with modern technologies to support business growth and additional features

### Target Audience
- Retail store owners and managers
- Cashiers and sales staff
- Business analysts requiring sales insights
- IT professionals managing POS infrastructure

## 2. Technical Architecture

### Component Breakdown
The ARHAT POS system follows a modern monorepo architecture with clear separation between frontend and backend components:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[ARHAT POS Monorepo] --> B[Web Application]
    A --> C[API Server]
    A --> D[Shared Packages]
    
    B --> B1[Next.js Frontend]
    B --> B2[React Components]
    B --> B3[State Management]
    
    C --> C1[Node.js API]
    C --> C2[Express Routes]
    C --> C3[Drizzle ORM]
    
    D --> D1[UI Components]
    D --> D2[TypeScript Config]
    D --> D3[ESLint Config]
```

### Design Patterns
- **Layered Architecture**: Clear separation between presentation, business logic, and data access layers
- **Repository Pattern**: Used extensively in data access layer for database operations
- **Service Layer Pattern**: Business logic encapsulated in dedicated service classes
- **Middleware Pattern**: Authentication and error handling implemented through middleware
- **Factory Pattern**: Dynamic service instantiation based on requirements

### System Relationships
The system operates through well-defined interfaces between components:

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Client
    participant WebApp
    participant APIServer
    participant Database
    
    Client->>+WebApp: User Action
    WebApp->>+APIServer: HTTP Request
    APIServer->>+Database: Query/Update
    Database-->>-APIServer: Response
    APIServer-->>-WebApp: JSON Response
    WebApp-->>-Client: Updated UI
```

### Data Flow Architecture
```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Client Device] --> B[Web Interface]
    B --> C[API Gateway]
    C --> D[Authentication Middleware]
    D --> E[Route Handlers]
    E --> F[Service Layer]
    F --> G[Database Layer]
    G --> H[(SQLite/PostgreSQL)]
    
    I[Mobile Device] --> J[POS Interface]
    J --> K[Transaction Service]
    K --> L[Inventory Service]
    L --> M[Analytics Service]
```

## 3. Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points
- **Web Application**: `apps/web/src/app/layout.tsx` - Next.js application entry point
- **API Server**: `apps/api/src/index.ts` - Node.js server initialization
- **Database Configuration**: `apps/api/drizzle.config.ts` - Drizzle ORM configuration

### Core Modules
- **Authentication System**: `apps/api/src/controllers/auth.controller.ts` & `apps/api/src/services/auth.service.ts`
- **Product Management**: `apps/api/src/controllers/product.controller.ts` & `apps/api/src/services/product.service.ts`
- **Inventory Control**: `apps/api/src/controllers/rawMaterial.controller.ts` & `apps/api/src/services/inventory.service.ts`
- **Transaction Processing**: `apps/api/src/controllers/transaction.controller.ts` & `apps/api/src/services/transaction.service.ts`
- **Analytics Engine**: `apps/api/src/controllers/analytics.controller.ts` & `apps/api/src/services/analytics.service.ts`

### Configuration Approach
- **Workspace Configuration**: `pnpm-workspace.yaml` - Monorepo workspace definition
- **Build Configuration**: `turbo.json` - Build system optimization
- **TypeScript Setup**: `apps/web/tsconfig.json` & `apps/api/tsconfig.json` - Language configuration
- **Environment Variables**: Managed through Vercel and Cloudflare Workers configuration

### External Dependencies
Key dependencies include:
- **Frontend**: Next.js 14+, React 18+, TypeScript
- **Backend**: Express.js, Drizzle ORM, SQLite/PostgreSQL
- **State Management**: Zustand for client-side state
- **UI Framework**: Tailwind CSS with custom component library
- **Development Tools**: ESLint, Prettier, Husky for Git hooks

### Integration Points
- **Cloudflare Workers**: Serverless deployment platform (`apps/api/wrangler.toml`)
- **Vercel Deployment**: Frontend hosting and CI/CD pipeline (`apps/api/vercel.json`)
- **WhatsApp Integration**: `apps/api/src/controllers/whatsapp.controller.ts` - Customer communication
- **Email Services**: `apps/api/src/services/email.service.ts` - Transaction notifications

### Component Relationship Diagram
```mermaid
graph LR
    A[Auth Context] --> B[Protected Routes]
    B --> C[Dashboard Layout]
    C --> D[POS Interface]
    C --> E[Inventory Management]
    C --> F[Customer Management]
    C --> G[Reports & Analytics]
    
    D --> H[Cart Store]
    E --> I[Product Service]
    F --> J[Customer Service]
    G --> K[Analytics Service]
```

## 4. Key Features

### Functionality Overview
The ARHAT POS system provides comprehensive retail management capabilities:

#### Core POS Features
- **Real-time Product Search**: Barcode scanning and text-based product lookup
- **Multi-payment Processing**: Cash, card, and digital payment methods
- **Inventory Tracking**: Automatic stock level updates during transactions
- **Shift Management**: Employee time tracking and sales reporting

#### Administrative Features
- **User Management**: Role-based access control and user permissions
- **Product Catalog**: Complete product management with variants and pricing
- **Supplier Integration**: Raw material tracking and supplier management
- **Reporting Dashboard**: Real-time sales analytics and inventory insights

### Implementation Highlights
- **PWA Support**: Progressive Web App functionality for offline operation
- **Barcode Integration**: Hardware barcode scanner compatibility
- **Image Management**: Product image upload and CDN integration
- **Cache Management**: Local storage optimization for performance

### Feature Architecture
```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Login
    Login --> Dashboard
    Dashboard --> POS
    Dashboard --> Inventory
    Dashboard --> Reports
    Dashboard --> Settings
    
    POS --> ProductSearch
    POS --> CartManagement
    POS --> PaymentProcessing
    POS --> TransactionComplete
    
    ProductSearch --> CartManagement
    CartManagement --> PaymentProcessing
    PaymentProcessing --> TransactionComplete
    TransactionComplete --> Dashboard
    
    Inventory --> ProductManagement
    Inventory --> StockTracking
    Inventory --> SupplierManagement
    
    Reports --> AnalyticsDashboard
    Reports --> ExportData
    
    Settings --> UserManagement
    Settings --> SystemConfiguration
```

### Technology Stack Integration
```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Frontend Layer] --> B[Next.js App Router]
    A --> C[React Components]
    A --> D[Zustand State]
    
    E[Backend Layer] --> F[Express Server]
    E --> G[Drizzle ORM]
    E --> H[Cloudflare Workers]
    
    I[Data Layer] --> J[SQLite Database]
    I --> K[Local IndexedDB Cache]
    
    L[External Services] --> M[WhatsApp API]
    L --> N[Email Services]
    L --> O[File Storage]
```

## Sources
- [package.json](apps/api/package.json)
- [layout.tsx](apps/web/src/app/layout.tsx)
- [index.ts](apps/api/src/index.ts)
- [auth.controller.ts](apps/api/src/controllers/auth.controller.ts)
- [product.controller.ts](apps/api/src/controllers/product.controller.ts)
- [transaction.controller.ts](apps/api/src/controllers/transaction.controller.ts)
- [analytics.controller.ts](apps/api/src/controllers/analytics.controller.ts)
- [wrangler.toml](apps/api/wrangler.toml)
- [vercel.json](apps/api/vercel.json)

</blog>
